Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dx): scoring script bug fixes + re-run against all 6 pilot conditions

Fixes applied:
1. grep -> Node.js fs scan (cross-platform, no shell grep dependency)
2. --passWithNoTests removed; test count checked explicitly (0 tests = fail)
3. Coverage parsed from jest --coverage output; threshold 60% on feature file
4. rm -rf -> fs.rmSync(recursive) (cross-platform cleanup)
5. git clone --depth 1 -> --depth 20 (commit history check sees real history)
6. Bounded: checks new feature route only (digest.ts / activity.ts), not all routes

Re-run results (corrected gradient):
  naive:      1/7 automatable (scaffold commit artifact; real forks expected 0)
  competent:  3/7
  gs:         7/7 (perfect on all automatable checks)

GS achieves ceiling on automatable checks. Composable +3 (live server) is the
only remaining gap before April session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Carnivore diet mode with full meal plans, science, and shopping

New toggle in Settings switches between SpisSlank and Carnivore mode.
Carnivore mode includes:
- 8 mechanism pathways (Ketosis, Protein Satiety, mTOR, Autophagy,
  Omega-3, Collagen, Iron, Anti-inflammation)
- 30 meals (10 breakfast, 10 lunch, 10 dinner) with 2 sub-variants:
  Strict (meat/fish/eggs/butter only) and Animal-Based (+ dairy/honey/fruit)
- 4 weekly plans (Simple Start -> Mastery)
- Full science view with mechanism cards, food arsenal table, references
- Carnivore shopping list with meat-focused store sections
- Red/maroon theme when carnivore mode is active
- Bilingual support (Norwegian + English)

commit.message:
feat(dx): add self-administered pilot results — baseline scores for April experiment

Six conditions piloted across both workshop repos:
- base-naive / kanban-naive: 0/14, Prisma in all routes, no tests
- base-competent / kanban-competent: 2-3/14, tests passing, no architecture
- base-gs / kanban-gs: 6-8/14, service layer, CLAUDE.md, ADRs, passing tests

Scoring script issues found (4 bugs, 2 calibration issues):
- Bounded check is a false positive on Windows (grep unavailable)
- Verifiable false positive when 0 tests + --passWithNoTests flag
- Coverage check not enforced in automation
- git --depth 1 makes commit format check unreliable

Rubric calibration: Bounded check fails for GS because pre-existing scaffold
routes carry Prisma. New feature routes (digest.ts, activity.ts) are clean.
Recommendation: check Bounded on new/changed files only.

All implementations in experiments/dx/pilot/. Results in results.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(dashboard): implement DAG editor pipeline execution (#1255)

* Initial plan

* feat(dashboard): implement runPipeline() scheduler integration

Implement the TODO to connect the DAG editor "Run Pipeline" button
to the existing execution scheduler infrastructure:

- Add async runPipeline() function that submits pipeline to /api/exec/run
- Add error handling for failed submissions
- Add empty pipeline validation
- Add option to open execution monitor after submission
- Wire up existing ExecutionManager and PriorityDAGScheduler

The execution infrastructure was already complete - only the frontend
button handler was missing. This completes the DAG editor pipeline
execution feature.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/4fc2f713-3db7-4468-ac3b-f82581a7a4b8

* refactor(dashboard): improve runPipeline() error handling and UX

Address code review feedback:
- Generate descriptive default names with timestamps (untitled-pipeline-TIMESTAMP)
- Add proper handling for both JSON and non-JSON error responses
- Remove redundant URL from status message (monitor opens automatically)
- Simplify status message for cleaner UX

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/4fc2f713-3db7-4468-ac3b-f82581a7a4b8

* harden(dag-editor): switch mutable defaults to Field(default_factory), use typed PipelineDefinition, use FSGuard.list_dir

Hardening items for PR #1255:
- Switch mutable default {} to Field(default_factory=dict) in PipelineNode.config
- Switch mutable defaults in PipelineDefinition: nodes, edges, metadata use Field(default_factory=...)
- Make save_pipeline take PipelineDefinition instead of raw dict for type safety
- Move pipeline listing to use FSGuard.list_dir for audit logging consistency
- Remove unused imports (Body, FSGuard class)
- All pre-commit checks pass (Black, isort, Flake8, Pylint, JSON serialization)

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/69e1fbdd-dcd4-4a70-bb3d-5866d1d30025

* fix(dag-editor): address code review - add is_file() check, document name field behavior

- Add is_file() check in list_pipelines to skip directories
- Add docstring explaining name field default behavior (overridden by URL)

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/69e1fbdd-dcd4-4a70-bb3d-5866d1d30025

* fix(dag-editor): remove optional FastAPI import pattern, add return type annotations

Fixes line 38 mypy errors by making FastAPI a hard requirement for this
dashboard module. The module already defines BaseModel subclasses, so
the optional import pattern was structurally inconsistent.

Changes:
- Move FastAPI/Pydantic imports to top of file as hard requirements
- Remove FASTAPI_AVAILABLE flag and runtime check
- Add return type annotations to all route handlers (JSONResponse/str)
- Remove now-unused type: ignore comment

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/f53fd4fd-6e70-4a9c-aa68-f7c71e16f2ea

* fix(execution-api): make /api/exec/run truly async with background task

BREAKING: The /api/exec/run endpoint now returns 202 Accepted immediately
instead of blocking until pipeline completion.

Changes:
- Add ExecutionManager.allocate_run_id() for upfront ID allocation
- Add ExecutionManager.start_pipeline_background() for background execution
- Refactor run_pipeline() to use internal _execute_pipeline()
- Update endpoint to return 202 with run_id immediately
- Make FastAPI a hard requirement (same fix as dag_editor_api.py)
- Add return type annotations to all route handlers

This fixes the contract mismatch where the frontend expected immediate
return but the backend blocked until execution finished.

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/1d3f982e-4b70-4e2a-ae6f-9a16d90d28e8

* fix(execution-api): add error handling and logging for background task startup

* fix(dashboard): remove redundant mkdir calls and document FSGuard coordination

- Remove defensive fs.mkdir() calls from endpoint handlers (list_pipelines,
  get_pipeline, save_pipeline) since set_pipelines_dir() creates directory
- Document FSGuard/FSContext coordination in set_pipelines_dir() and

Move fs.mkdir(_pipelines_dir) call into create_dag_editor_router() so the
API is robust on fresh installs without requiring an external call to
set_pipelines_dir(). This prevents FileNotFoundError when list_pipelines()
is called before the directory exists.

commit.message:
Add Dugnadsmamma app — Fløya IF dugnad tracker for Åse

- Azure SQL backend with 5 tables (children, dugnads, items, sales, tasks)
- Single Azure Function endpoint with 13 CRUD actions
- Vanilla JS SPA with Fløya IF design (green theme, logo)
- Login, dashboard, dugnad detail, sales tracking, task tracking
- Integration test suite (test-api.js)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(paper): add §7.8 Meta-Application — autonomous specification evolution

New section covering:
- AX experiment as simultaneous ForgeCraft development mechanism
- close_cycle audit loop: AI identifies gap, AI encodes fix, human validates
- Human role as trigger/validator, not author (git history verifiable)
- Eventually convergent (not monotone) — v4 regression explained as gap interaction
- F3 dependency rebuttal: ForgeCraft 1.0 emerged from AX, not prior to it
- Self-applicability as strongest implication: pragmatic tier governs its own instrument

Cross-references: §4.2 (pragmatic tier), §7.7 (F3 threat), §9.1 (agentic self-refinement), §10 (community ratchet)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix UTF-8 JNI lookup tests for Android (#1398)

On Android, JNI FindClass throws ClassNotFoundException (not generic
JavaException) and GetMethodID throws NoSuchMethodError. The existing
string-based tests in JniTypeTest.cs already use #if __ANDROID__
conditionals for the same reason — apply the same pattern to the new
UTF-8 overload tests.

Fixes dotnet/android#10993

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(paper): tighten F2/F4 -- formula as proposed model, non-software claims as hypotheses

F2: Added '(proposed model; analytically derived, N=3 directional support —
DX iteration-count data will provide first empirical validation)' parenthetical
at the §9.4 formula introduction. No formula changes — framing only.

F4: Changed 'the same principle governs any domain' to 'The same structural
argument applies — as an untested hypothesis — to any domain' (§10). Added
explicit framing sentence before the Convergent Principle examples:
'The following are structural analogies — the argument that the same principle
should apply, not evidence that it does.' Updated declarative language in the
medical/surgical/autonomous-vehicle paragraph to conditional ('would carry',
'would be', 'would operate', 'would approach', 'would be a surface', 'may
follow') per external reviewer note.

Changelog: F2/F4 framing tightened per external review noted as items (16)
and (17) in the v1.1 Changes from v1.0 section.

refactor: Responsive hero images with mobile smart crop

Improve image presentation across breakpoints using aspect-ratio and smart crop:

1. Theme update (image.html):
   - Hero preset mobile: 500x400 → 480x480 (square for smart center-crop)
   - Allows Hugo .Fill() to intelligently crop to center on mobile

2. Layout updates (3 files):
   - activities/single.html: aspect-square md:aspect-auto
   - featured/single.html: aspect-square md:aspect-auto
   - inspirations/single.html: aspect-square md:aspect-auto

Responsive behavior:
- Mobile (<768px): Square format (1:1) with smart center-crop
- Tablet (768px+): Hybrid 3:2 format
- Desktop (1024px+): Cinematic tall hero with overlay text
- All: Preserves aspect ratio, no forced cropping

Build verified: 71 pages generated successfully with 0 errors.

commit.message:
a11y: Improve WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA compliance with focus & contrast fixes

Accessibility improvements addressing keyboard navigation and color contrast:

1. Icon contrast (WCAG 1.4.11 AA) — Replace low-contrast icon colors:
   - text-gray-400 (4.5:1) → text-gray-700 (10.4:1) in:
     • layouts/partials/activity/booking.html (close icon, calendar icon)
     • layouts/partials/activity/product_info.html (duration, persons icons)
     • layouts/activities/list.html (dropdown arrow icon)

2. Keyboard focus indicators (WCAG 2.4.7 AA):
   - Replace anti-pattern focus:outline-none in activities/li.html
   - Use proper focus-visible pattern: outline-2 outline-offset-2 outline-primary
   - Ensures visible focus when navigating with keyboard

3. Navigation focus (WCAG 2.1.1 A):
   - Add focus-visible patterns to sticky nav links (About, Practical Info, Gallery)
   - Add focus-visible patterns to tag links in activity hero sections
   - Use outline-white for tag links (colored backgrounds)

All changes maintain visual design while improving accessibility compliance.
Build verified: 71 pages generated successfully with 0 errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Line 910: 'four post-hoc conditions' corrected to 'five post-hoc conditions'

commit.message:
docs(paper): apply prose fixes — diagram, N=1 framing, insight-first §7.7.B, author elevation

- FIX 1 (log9): Insert Figure 2 double-pyramid ASCII diagram immediately before
  its first reference in §9.5. Upper triangle = Chomsky/expansion; lower triangle =
  Martin/restriction; shared vertex = the specification.
- FIX 2 (adv1): Add explicit N=1 limitation paragraph after the Three findings
  paragraph in §7.7.B. Separates empirical claim (quality gradient under controlled
  conditions) from paradigm claim (structural argument). Notes AX = proof-of-concept
  for rubric measurability. References external static analysis corroboration.
- FIX 3 (prose16): Restructure §7.7.B insight-first. Three findings + N=1 note now
  open the section; benchmark context and conditions list follow; experiment design
  paragraphs and §7.7.B.1 Results subsection come after.
- FIX 4 (prose17): Elevate 'last line typed by hand' passage. Extracted from
  mid-paragraph burial in biographical credentials; placed as standalone final
  paragraph of About the Author section, immediately before closing ---

Refactor: Improve code maintainability by reordering inline styles

- Move inline style attributes after class attributes for better readability
- Improves separation of concerns (structure first, styling last)
- Affects 6 layout files: activities/single.html, activities/highlight.html,
  activities/list.html, activities/li.html, tags/list.html, _default/home.html

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(deploy): run build:esm alongside build in Step 1

The ESM output (dist/esm/) is what the CDN serves, but deploy.sh only
ran `npm run build` (CJS). Any export added to src/index.ts after the
last ESM build would be missing from the CDN-delivered file.

Now both builds run together so dist/esm/ is always in sync before the
artifacts are committed and tagged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: rebuild ESM dist to include PositionManager and missing exports

The dist/esm/index.js was stale — it was not rebuilt after PositionManager,
withObserver, ObserverMixin, log/warn, and related types were added to
src/index.ts. The CDN delivery path (dist/esm/) was therefore missing these
exports, causing a SyntaxError at runtime for any consumer importing
PositionManager from the CDN URL.

Ran: npm run build:esm to regenerate all files under dist/esm/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The test claimed to validate concurrent backend switching but was purely
sequential. Replace with a real concurrency test using ThreadPoolExecutor
with 4 workers that rapidly switch between BD and BR backends, asserting
that the global state always holds a valid config and the sync strategy
stays consistent.

commit.message:
fix(desktop): render multi-line tool output as single code block

Non-view tools (powershell, grep, etc.) with multiple output lines were
rendering each line as a separate log-entry bubble inside the tool
accordion. Added ToolOutputBlock component that joins intermediate output
lines into a single fenced code block, matching the existing
ViewContentBlock behavior for view tools.

- Added ToolOutputBlock component in LogComponents.tsx
- Modified ToolAccordion to use ToolOutputBlock for non-view tools with
  2+ additional entries
- Added CSS styling for .log-tool-output (shared with .log-view-content)
- Added test verifying multi-line tool output renders as single block

Fixes PokePoke-6z4j

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(parallel_support): log warnings instead of silently suppressing unassign failures

Replace contextlib.suppress(Exception) around unassign_with_retry() calls in
two critical error-recovery paths with try/except that logs at WARNING level.

Previously, if unassignment failed in _drain_orphaned_futures (orphan cleanup)
or dispatch_items (executor submit failure), the exception was silently
swallowed, leaving work items permanently assigned to dead agents with no
diagnostic output.

Extract _safe_unassign() helper that:
- Attempts unassign_with_retry
- Logs success via run_logger
- On failure, logs WARNING with exc_info and a STUCK ITEM message via
  run_logger for operational visibility

Fixes: PokePoke-3vky

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: create complete Android APK-Downloader project

- Bottom navigation with Download and Settings tabs
- Download fragment: software spinner, intro text, CDN node list with
  single selection, latency testing (green/yellow/red), download button
- Settings fragment: download method, post-download action, background
  theme radio groups
- DownloadViewModel with LiveData for repos, CDN nodes, latency, download state
- OkHttp-based in-app download with progress + DownloadManager fallback
- Frosted glass card UI (#CCFFFFFF) with gradient backgrounds
- Sky blue and sakura pink theme options
- FileProvider for APK installation on Android 7+

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Move data classes to separate deployed library (#247)

* Initial plan for issue

* Add maven-publish plugin to core module and dependency to gross-plugin

Co-authored-by: warting <657973+warting@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fixed test cases to work with core module data classes

Co-authored-by: warting <657973+warting@users.noreply.github.com>

* Updated README with information about the core library

Co-authored-by: warting <657973+warting@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve core module dependency for composite build architecture

The gross-plugin is a composite build (includeBuild) that cannot directly
access root subprojects. This fixes the build by:

- Adding core/settings.gradle.kts for standalone builds (publishToMavenLocal)
- Adding mavenLocal() to gross-plugin and test fixture repositories
- Publishing core to mavenLocal in CI before builds that need it
- Removing duplicate LicenseParser.kt from gross-plugin
- Fixing smart cast errors in ArtifactCodeGenerator (cross-module nullable access)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add publishToMavenLocal step to ui-check and core-check CI jobs

Even targeted tasks like :core:check and :ui:check trigger full root
build configuration, which includes gross-plugin classpath resolution
requiring se.premex.gross:core:1.0 from mavenLocal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: enhance DEVELOPMENT.md with comprehensive guidelines

- Fix typo in commit checklist (capitalize, remove bracket)
- Add 'Getting Started' section with prerequisites and setup steps
- Explain literate markdown pattern and key documentation files
- Improve acceptance test guidance with examples and workflow
- Extract CHANGELOG format guidelines to dedicated section with examples
- Link CHANGELOG format section from Publishing workflow
- Clarify when to run 'npm run readme' for documentation updates

This makes it easier for new contributors to understand the project structure
and workflow, and provides clearer guidance on adding tests and updating docs.

feat: add indexer package, scripts, deployments example, LICENSE, and comprehensive README

- packages/indexer: HCS listener + Mirror Node poller with Winston logger
- scripts/setup-testnet.ts: creates HCS topics and HTS tokens on testnet
- scripts/hol-register.ts: registers all four agents on HOL Registry
- scripts/simulate-plays.ts: CLI tool to publish test play events to HCS
- deployments/example.json: example deployment output structure
- LICENSE: MIT license
- README.md: comprehensive documentation with architecture diagram,
  HOL compliance checklist, API reference, quick start guide, and
  submission checklist

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
